title: APEX IaC Contract
description: Machine-readable contract emitted by Step 4 (05-IaC Planner) and consumed by Step 5 (06b/06t CodeGen). Replaces prose extraction from 04-implementation-plan.md. Two revisions are supported: contract-v0 (minimum viable — resources, modules, params, diagnostics, identity, l1m_ref) ships in Wave 1; contract-v1 (adds dependencies, network_topology, output_contract) ships in Wave 3. Artifact path: agent-output/{project}/04-iac-contract.json. Validators: validate-iac-contract.mjs (schema), validate-iac-contract-consistency.mjs (prose ↔ contract drift).

$schema.description: Optional JSON-Schema reference for editor tooling.

schema_version.description: v0 = Wave-1 minimum viable; v1 = Wave-3 full contract with dependencies, network_topology, and output_contract.

plan_ref.description: Pointer to the source 04-implementation-plan.md and its content hash, so 06b/06t can detect plan drift between contract emission and code generation.

resources.description: Every resource the CodeGen agent must materialise. logical_name is the contract-stable identifier (lowercase, dash-separated). type uses the Azure provider namespace (e.g. Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts).

sku.description: SKU/tier identifier (e.g. Standard_LRS, Standard_B2ms). Null when the resource type has no SKU concept (e.g. resource groups).

tags_inherit.description: When true, the resource inherits required tags from the subscription/resource-group scope; when false, the contract caller is responsible for asserting tags explicitly.

depends_on.description: logical_name references of resources this one depends on. Optional in v0, recommended in v1.

modules.description: AVM (Azure Verified Modules) and AVM-TF references the CodeGen agent must use. bicep[].source uses br/public:avm/res/* registry paths; terraform[].source uses Azure/avm-res-*/azurerm Registry paths. Pinned versions only — never ranges.

source.description: Either Azure/avm-res-*/azurerm Registry path or a verified Git source URL pinned to a tag.

params.description: Parameters the CodeGen agent must surface on the root template. NEVER includes secrets or environment-specific values — those live in the environment manifest.

default.description: Optional default value. Must be a non-secret literal.

diagnostics.description: Diagnostic settings strategy. mode=avm relies on the AVM module's built-in diagnosticSettings property (preferred). mode=module falls back to a project-owned diagnostics module per resource when an AVM does not expose diagnosticSettings. mode=auto lets the CodeGen agent pick per resource based on AVM capability — recorded in 05-iac-handoff.json.

workspace_id_param.description: Name of the param that supplies the Log Analytics workspace resource ID.

log_categories.description: Either explicit category names or the string 'allLogs' for AVM passthrough.

identity.description: Identity contract for the workload. UAMI logical names map to resources[].logical_name entries of type Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities. system_assigned applies to compute resources individually via per-resource flags emitted by CodeGen.

entra_app_creation.description: When 'create', Terraform may use azuread_application — gated behind a permission preflight and prevent_destroy=true.

entra_app_existing_id_param.description: Name of the param supplying an existing app registration object ID (required when entra_app_creation == existing AND an app reg is needed).

l1m_ref.description: Pointer to the machine-readable governance compliance matrix (04-policy-property-map.json) that the CodeGen agent must honour. L1m is mandatory in both light and full governance modes.

dependencies.description: v1 only — explicit cross-resource dependency edges beyond what depends_on captures, for graph-based ordering.

network_topology.description: v1 only — topology hints (hub-spoke ids, vnet/subnet logical names) so CodeGen can compose private-endpoint and route-table patterns deterministically.

output_contract.description: v1 only — outputs the deployment must expose so downstream as-built (Step 7) and policy precheck (L3) can attest without re-reading the IaC tree.

pinPolicy.description: Structured exception for non-latest AVM module pins. When mode='latest' the validator only checks the version exists; when mode='exception' the validator allows a stale pin if rationale + evidence + review_after are present and review_after is in the future. Enforced by validate-avm-module-versions.mjs.

mode.description: 'latest' = the pinned version was the latest stable at lookup_timestamp. 'exception' = an older verified version is intentionally pinned with rationale.

latest_seen.description: Latest stable version observed at lookup_timestamp. Should equal the module's `version` field when mode='latest'.

lookup_source.description: Where latest_seen came from. 'cache' means the resolver used the checked-in cache file because the live registry was unreachable.

lookup_timestamp.description: When latest_seen was observed. Validators may require this be within an acceptable freshness window.

rationale.description: Required when mode='exception'. One-sentence reason for not using latest (e.g. 'AVM 0.12.x emits networkRuleSet for Basic SKU; pin to 0.11.x until 0.13.x ships the fix').

evidence_url_or_file.description: Required when mode='exception'. Link to GitHub issue, release note, or repo path documenting the regression.

review_after.description: Required when mode='exception'. Date after which the exception must be re-evaluated. Past dates make the validator fail.

approved_by_step.description: Optional. Workflow step that approved the exception (typically 4 for plan-time decisions).
